text:
areya Brdhmana speaks of the Kuru-Pdhcdla country as com-
prising the Vaias and UHnaras. To the south of this Middle Country
lay the Sdtvatas', to (he east, the Prdcyas i.e. Kdii, Kosala, Videha, and
Magadha; to the west, the Nicyas and Apdcyas; and to the north, the
Himalayas, beyond which the Uttarakurus and Uttaramadras (Olden-
berg). According to Oldenberg the testimony of the Rgveda shows
the Bharatas as the rulers of the regions around the -Sarasvati and
Dffadvati.
In chapter IV the author mentions a number of kingdoms and
peoples : Pdn4yas, Pratyantus, Magadha, Tavanas, Pulindas, Nepdla,
Bhrngi, Maru, Kaccha, Surdstra, Madra, Pdncdla, Kekaya, KulUta, Puru-
Sdda, Usinaras, Gandhara, Sauviras, Sindhu, Kiras, Dravifa, Trigarta,
Mdlava, Kulinda, Sibi, Ayodhya, Kaurava, Matsya, Sukti, Taudheyas,
Prdcyas, Arjundyanas, Mathurd and banks of the Vend, these being
assigned to different quarters. Parasara, however, assigns Dravidas
to the east.
